tests.title: All 3 services start and stay up (control-plane port 3200, slack-relay port 3101, scheduler)
steps: Run start command for control-plane, slack-relay, and scheduler via process manager
Wait 10 seconds
Check port bindings: ss -tlnp | grep -E '3200|3101'
Check scheduler process: pgrep -f scheduler
Wait 60 seconds, then re-check all three
passFail: All processes running; ports 3200 and 3101 bound; no unexpected exits in logs after 60 seconds

required: Process list output showing all 3 services
Port binding output showing 3200 and 3101 bound
Service logs with no ERROR or exit lines

tests.title: Services survive process manager restart without EADDRINUSE port conflicts
steps: Confirm all services running (TC-001 must pass first)
Run process manager restart: pm2 restart all
Wait 15 seconds
Scan logs for EADDRINUSE: pm2 logs --lines 100 | grep -i EADDRINUSE
Confirm ports re-bound: ss -tlnp | grep -E '3200|3101'
commands: pm2 logs --lines 100 | grep -i EADDRINUSE || echo 'PASS: no EADDRINUSE'
passFail: No EADDRINUSE errors in logs after restart
Ports 3200 and 3101 re-bound successfully within 15 seconds

required: PM restart log output
grep EADDRINUSE output (empty = pass)
Port check output showing bindings restored

tests.title: Services load .env config — Zod validation passes, no 'Invalid input: expected string' errors
steps: Start all services fresh from clean state
Wait 5 seconds for startup to complete
Grep service logs for Zod validation error string
Confirm each service log shows 'started' or 'config loaded' without validation errors
commands: pm2 logs --lines 200 | grep 'Invalid input: expected string' || echo 'PASS: no Zod errors'
grep -r 'Invalid input: expected string' /var/log/claw/ 2>/dev/null || echo 'PASS: no Zod errors in log dir'
passFail: No 'Invalid input: expected string' lines in any service log
Each service log contains startup confirmation line

required: Log grep output (empty = pass)
Startup confirmation lines from each service log

tests.title: Provisioning creates /data/tenants/<id>/ directories with correct permissions (writable by UID 1001)
steps: Trigger provisioning for test user via control-plane API or relay DM event
Capture tenant ID from DB: sqlite3 /data/claw.db "SELECT id FROM tenants ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 1;"
Check directory existence and permissions: ls -la /data/tenants/<id>/
Test write access as UID 1001: docker run --rm -u 1001 -v /data/tenants/<id>:/t alpine sh -c 'touch /t/probe && rm /t/probe'
commands: TENANT_ID=$(sqlite3 /data/claw.db "SELECT id FROM tenants ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 1;")
docker run --rm -u 1001 -v /data/tenants/${TENANT_ID}:/t alpine sh -c 'touch /t/probe && rm /t/probe && echo PASS: writable by uid 1001'
passFail: Directory exists at /data/tenants/<id>/
Docker write probe exits 0 (writable by UID 1001)

required: ls -la output showing directory with owner and permissions
Docker write probe exit code = 0 and PASS message

tests.title: Docker run succeeds: all bind mounts correct, container starts and stays running (not OOM, not permission errors)
steps: Run docker container for a test tenant with required bind mounts
Wait 10 seconds for container to stabilize
Check container status: docker inspect <id> --format '{{.State.Status}}'
Check OOM killed flag: docker inspect <id> --format '{{.State.OOMKilled}}'
Check for permission errors: docker logs <id> 2>&1 | grep -i 'permission denied'
commands: CONTAINER_ID=$(sqlite3 /data/claw.db "SELECT container_id FROM tenants ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 1;")
docker logs ${CONTAINER_ID} 2>&1 | grep -i 'permission denied' || echo 'PASS: no permission errors'
passFail: Container status = 'running'
OOMKilled = false
No 'permission denied' lines in container logs

required: docker inspect status output (running)
docker inspect OOMKilled output (false)
docker logs grep output (empty = pass)

tests.title: OpenClaw gateway starts inside container (openclaw gateway start runs without crashing)
steps: Identify running tenant container ID from DB
Check gateway status inside container: docker exec <id> openclaw gateway status
If not running, start it: docker exec <id> openclaw gateway start
Check exit code: echo $?
Re-verify status: docker exec <id> openclaw gateway status
commands: CONTAINER_ID=$(sqlite3 /data/claw.db "SELECT container_id FROM tenants WHERE status='ACTIVE' LIMIT 1;")
docker exec ${CONTAINER_ID} openclaw gateway status || true
docker exec ${CONTAINER_ID} openclaw gateway start; echo "exit:$?"
passFail: openclaw gateway start exits with code 0
Subsequent status check shows gateway active/running

required: gateway start command exit code = 0
gateway status output showing active/running state

tests.title: Health server responds at container's port 3101
steps: Identify host port mapped to container port 3101: docker inspect <id> --format '{{json .NetworkSettings.Ports}}'
Send health check request: curl -sf http://localhost:<host-port>/health
Check HTTP status code: curl -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' http://localhost:<host-port>/health
commands: CONTAINER_ID=$(sqlite3 /data/claw.db "SELECT container_id FROM tenants WHERE status='ACTIVE' LIMIT 1;")
passFail: HTTP status code = 200
Response body is non-empty

required: curl HTTP status code output (200)
curl response body (non-empty)
docker inspect port mapping output

tests.title: Incoming DM from allowlisted Slack user triggers provisioning and bot replies
steps: Send simulated Slack DM event to relay endpoint
Check relay response HTTP code
Poll DB for tenant creation: sqlite3 /data/claw.db query
Check relay outbound log for bot reply event
commands: sqlite3 /data/claw.db "SELECT id, status, slack_user_id FROM tenants ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 1;"
passFail: Relay returns HTTP 200
Tenant row created in DB with status ACTIVE for allowlisted user
Relay log shows bot reply event dispatched

required: curl HTTP response code = 200 and response body
sqlite3 query result showing new tenant row with ACTIVE status
relay log grep output showing reply event

tests.title: Message delivered to container and response returned to Slack user
steps: After TC-008, send a follow-up message payload to relay
Wait for processing
Check container received message via workspace log
Check relay outbound log for response sent back to user
commands: CONTAINER_ID=$(sqlite3 /data/claw.db "SELECT container_id FROM tenants WHERE status='ACTIVE' ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 1;")
passFail: Message content appears in container workspace log or container stdout
Relay log shows outbound message dispatched to correct Slack user ID

required: Container message log or docker logs showing received message
Relay outbound log grep showing response dispatched to user

tests.title: auth-profiles.json and .credentials.json readable inside container at /home/agent/ paths
steps: Identify running tenant container ID
Check file existence and permissions: docker exec <id> ls -la /home/agent/auth-profiles.json /home/agent/.credentials.json
Verify readability: docker exec <id> cat /home/agent/auth-profiles.json | head -5
Verify .credentials.json: docker exec <id> cat /home/agent/.credentials.json | head -5
commands: CONTAINER_ID=$(sqlite3 /data/claw.db "SELECT container_id FROM tenants WHERE status='ACTIVE' ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 1;")
passFail: Both files listed at /home/agent/ paths with readable permissions
cat exits 0 for both files
Content is non-empty (first 5 lines non-blank)

required: ls -la output showing both files with readable permissions
cat output for auth-profiles.json (first 5 lines)
cat output for .credentials.json (first 5 lines)

tests.title: Second message from same user reuses tenant (no re-provisioning)
steps: After TC-008 establishes tenant, record original tenant ID from DB
Send second DM payload for same user
Query DB for tenant count for that user
Verify same tenant ID and no new container spawned
commands: ORIGINAL_ID=$(sqlite3 /data/claw.db "SELECT id FROM tenants WHERE slack_user_id='TEST_USER_ID' ORDER BY created_at ASC LIMIT 1;")
sqlite3 /data/claw.db "SELECT COUNT(*) FROM tenants WHERE slack_user_id='TEST_USER_ID';"
sqlite3 /data/claw.db "SELECT id FROM tenants WHERE slack_user_id='TEST_USER_ID' ORDER BY created_at ASC LIMIT 1;"
passFail: Tenant count for user = 1 (no duplicate rows)
Tenant ID after second message matches original tenant ID
No new container started for this user

required: sqlite3 count result = 1
sqlite3 ID comparison showing same tenant ID
docker ps output showing no duplicate container for user

tests.title: Unauthorized user receives rejection message
steps: Send DM event from non-allowlisted user to relay
Check relay logs for rejection response
Confirm no tenant row created for unauthorized user in DB
commands: sqlite3 /data/claw.db "SELECT COUNT(*) FROM tenants WHERE slack_user_id='UNAUTHORIZED_USER_ID';"
passFail: Relay logs show rejection/unauthorized message dispatched to user
DB tenant count for unauthorized user = 0

required: curl response body and status code
relay log grep showing rejection message sent
sqlite3 count result = 0 for unauthorized user

tests.title: AGENTS.md pre-seeded in /workspace with Task Execution section
steps: Trigger provisioning for a fresh tenant (or use existing active tenant)
Check AGENTS.md file exists inside container: docker exec <id> ls -la /workspace/AGENTS.md
Verify Task Execution section present: docker exec <id> grep -n 'Task Execution' /workspace/AGENTS.md
commands: CONTAINER_ID=$(sqlite3 /data/claw.db "SELECT container_id FROM tenants WHERE status='ACTIVE' ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 1;")
passFail: /workspace/AGENTS.md exists inside container
grep returns at least one line matching 'Task Execution'

required: ls -la output confirming AGENTS.md at /workspace/
grep output with line number and 'Task Execution' match

tests.title: Bot sends 'working on it' interim message after 15 seconds
steps: Send a message expected to take >15 seconds to process
Record send timestamp
Wait 20 seconds
Check relay outbound log for interim message containing expected text
Verify timestamp of interim message is 15-30 seconds after send
commands: INTERIM_TS=$(grep -i 'working on it' /var/log/claw/relay.log | tail -1 | grep -oP '\d{10}' | head -1); echo "delay: $((INTERIM_TS - START_TS)) seconds"
passFail: Relay log contains 'working on it' (or equivalent interim message) entry
Interim message timestamp is between 15 and 30 seconds after initial send

required: grep output showing 'working on it' log entry with timestamp
Calculated delay in seconds (must be 15-30)

tests.title: Tenant state persists across control plane restart
steps: Confirm tenant active in DB (from TC-008)
Record tenant ID and status before restart
Restart control-plane only: pm2 restart control-plane
Wait 10 seconds for restart to complete
Query tenant state from DB post-restart
Send another message and confirm routing works
commands: sqlite3 /data/claw.db "SELECT id, status FROM tenants WHERE status='ACTIVE' ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 1;"
sqlite3 /data/claw.db "SELECT id, status FROM tenants ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 1;"
passFail: Tenant row exists in DB post-restart with same ID and status
Message routing still functional after control-plane restart (HTTP 200 from relay)

required: sqlite3 query before restart showing tenant ID and status
sqlite3 query after restart showing same tenant ID and status
curl response code = 200 confirming routing works post-restart

tests.title: Services survive server reboot
steps: Reboot host or simulate via process manager: sudo reboot
Wait for system to come back online (poll SSH availability)
Check all 3 services running: pm2 list or systemctl status
Confirm ports rebound: ss -tlnp | grep -E '3200|3101'
passFail: All 3 services appear in pm2 list or systemctl status as running after reboot
Ports 3200 and 3101 re-bound without manual intervention

required: pm2 list or systemctl status output post-reboot showing all 3 services running
Port binding output showing 3200 and 3101 bound

tests.title: Container OOM is detected and tenant marked UNHEALTHY
steps: Identify active tenant container
Trigger OOM by running memory exhaustion inside container or lowering memory limit
Wait for OOM kill event
Check OOM flag: docker inspect <id> --format '{{.State.OOMKilled}}'
Poll tenant DB for status update: sqlite3 query for UNHEALTHY
commands: CONTAINER_ID=$(sqlite3 /data/claw.db "SELECT container_id FROM tenants WHERE status='ACTIVE' ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 1;")
TENANT_ID=$(sqlite3 /data/claw.db "SELECT id FROM tenants WHERE status='ACTIVE' ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 1;")
sqlite3 /data/claw.db "SELECT status FROM tenants WHERE id='${TENANT_ID}';"
passFail: docker inspect OOMKilled = true
Tenant status in DB = UNHEALTHY within 30 seconds of OOM event

required: docker inspect OOMKilled = true output
sqlite3 tenant status = UNHEALTHY output

tests.title: Stopped tenant wakes on next message
steps: Confirm a tenant exists in STOPPED state, or manually stop: docker stop <id> and update DB
Send DM for that tenant's user
Poll container status: docker inspect <tenant-container-id> --format '{{.State.Status}}'
Confirm message delivered after wake
commands: TENANT_ID=$(sqlite3 /data/claw.db "SELECT id FROM tenants WHERE status='STOPPED' LIMIT 1;")
CONTAINER_ID=$(sqlite3 /data/claw.db "SELECT container_id FROM tenants WHERE id='${TENANT_ID}';")
sqlite3 /data/claw.db "SELECT status FROM tenants WHERE id='${TENANT_ID}';"
passFail: Container transitions from stopped to running within 10 seconds of message
Tenant status in DB updated to ACTIVE
Message delivery confirmed in relay logs

required: docker inspect status = running post-wake
sqlite3 tenant status = ACTIVE
relay log showing message delivered after wake
